fsm_model.section_ref: Matter Specification R1.4, Document 23-27349, Section 11.24, Pages 1017-1047
fsm_model.description: Complete Finite State Machine for Joint Fabric Datastore managing Nodes, Groups, and Group membership in Joint Fabric. Provides centralized authority for fabric topology, access control policies, and cryptographic key distribution to all fabric members. Covers all 20 commands from specification: AddAdmin, UpdateAdmin, RemoveAdmin, AddKeySet, UpdateKeySet, RemoveKeySet, AddGroup, UpdateGroup, RemoveGroup, AddPendingNode, RefreshNode, UpdateNode, RemoveNode, UpdateEndpointForNode, AddGroupIDToEndpointForNode, RemoveGroupIDFromEndpointForNode, AddACLToNode, RemoveACLFromNode, AddBindingToEndpointForNode, RemoveBindingFromEndpointForNode.

states.name: FabricNotFormed
states.description: Joint Fabric has not been established. No administrators or anchor root CA configured.

states.name: FabricPending
states.description: Joint Fabric initialization in progress. Anchor administrator added but datastore not yet ready for operations.

states.name: FabricCommitted
states.description: Joint Fabric fully operational. Datastore ready to accept node and group management commands.

states.name: FabricTransferring
states.description: Anchor administrator transfer in progress. Datastore being migrated to new anchor administrator.

states.name: NodeAbsent
states.description: Node does not have entry in datastore NodeList. Not commissioned or has been removed.

states.name: NodePending
states.description: Node entry created in datastore with Pending status. Commissioning in progress but not yet completed.

states.name: NodeCommitted
states.description: Node successfully commissioned and synchronized. All configurations match between datastore and actual node.
invariants: all entries in NodeKeySetList have State == Committed
all entries in ACLList have State == Committed
all entries in EndpointList.GroupIDList have State == Committed
all entries in EndpointList.BindingList have State == Committed

states.name: NodePartiallyConfigured
states.description: Node commissioned but has pending or delete-pending updates. Awaiting synchronization via RefreshNode.
invariants: exists entry in (NodeKeySetList OR ACLList OR EndpointList.GroupIDList OR EndpointList.BindingList) with State == Pending OR State == DeletePending

states.name: GroupAbsent
states.description: Group does not exist in datastore GroupList.

states.name: GroupActive
states.description: Group exists and operational. May have zero or more member nodes, all with Committed or Pending memberships.

states.name: GroupBeingRemoved
states.description: Group exists but one or more member nodes have DeletePending status for this group. Awaiting final removal.

states.name: KeySetAbsent
states.description: KeySet does not exist in GroupKeySetList.

states.name: KeySetActive
states.description: KeySet exists in GroupKeySetList and may be used by groups.

states.name: IPKActive
states.description: Identity Protection Key (IPK) with ID 0 exists. This state is mandatory and permanent.

states.name: EntryPending_Generic
states.description: Generic state for any sub-entry (KeySet on node, ACL on node, GroupID on endpoint, Binding on endpoint) with Status Pending.
invariants: update to node attempted or not yet attempted

states.name: EntryCommitted_Generic
states.description: Generic state for any sub-entry with Status Committed. Entry exists in both datastore and actual node.

states.name: EntryDeletePending_Generic
states.description: Generic state for any sub-entry with Status DeletePending. Removal from node attempted, awaiting confirmation.

states.name: ErrorState_ConstraintViolation
states.description: Operation attempted that violates datastore constraints (duplicate ID, resource in use, IPK removal).

states.name: ErrorState_NotFound
states.description: Operation attempted on non-existent entity.

states.name: ErrorState_ResourceExhausted
states.description: Storage capacity limit reached. No new entries can be added until space freed.

transitions.guard_condition: AdminList.length >= 1 && AnchorRootCA != null && GroupKeySetList contains IPK
node_exists(NodeID) && endpoint_exists(NodeID, EndpointID) && groupid_exists_in_endpoint(NodeID, EndpointID, GroupID)
node_exists(NodeID) && endpoint_exists(NodeID, EndpointID) && binding_exists_in_endpoint(NodeID, EndpointID, ListID)

actions: create_group_entry(GroupID, FriendlyName, GroupKeySetID, GroupCAT, GroupCATVersion, GroupPermission)

transitions.guard_condition: exists entries with State == Pending || State == DeletePending

functions.name: add_admin_to_list

parameters.name: AdminInformationEntry
parameters.description: Administrator information containing NodeID, FriendlyName, VendorID, ICAC

functions.description: Adds administrator entry to AdminList. First administrator added becomes anchor (index 0).
functions.algorithm_detail: Insert AdminInformationEntry into AdminList. If list was empty, assign index=0 and mark as anchor administrator. Otherwise assign next available index >= 1.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T001 when first administrator forms fabric

functions.name: derive_root_ca
functions.description: Extracts or derives the Root CA certificate from administrator's ICAC chain.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse X.509 ICAC certificate from AdminInformationEntry.ICAC field. Extract issuer certificate or derive root CA from certificate chain. Returns root CA certificate in DER-encoded format.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T001 to establish AnchorRootCA when fabric is formed

functions.name: add_ipk_to_keysetlist
functions.description: Adds Identity Protection Key (IPK) to GroupKeySetList with ID 0. This is mandatory for all fabrics.
functions.algorithm_detail: Create GroupKeySetStruct with GroupKeySetID=0, populate with fabric's IPK epoch key material. Insert into GroupKeySetList at index corresponding to ID 0. IPK is used for identity protection in all fabric communications.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T001 and T034 during fabric formation to establish mandatory IPK

functions.name: create_node_entry

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the node being added

functions.description: Creates new Node Information Entry in NodeList with initial state.
functions.algorithm_detail: Allocate new NodeInformationEntry structure. Set NodeID, FriendlyName. Initialize empty NodeKeySetList, ACLList, EndpointList. Set CommissioningStatusEntry.State to Pending. Insert into NodeList.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T005 when AddPendingNode command adds node to datastore

functions.name: synchronize_endpoint_list

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node whose endpoints are being synchronized

functions.description: Synchronizes datastore's endpoint list with actual node's PartsList from Descriptor cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Read PartsList from node's Descriptor cluster using read_partslist_from_node(). For each endpoint in datastore not in PartsList: remove from datastore. For each endpoint in PartsList: ensure exists in datastore. For each endpoint's GroupIDList entries with Status=Pending: push to node, mark Committed if success. For entries with Status=DeletePending: remove from node, delete entry if success. Same process for BindingList entries.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T007, T015 during RefreshNode to ensure endpoint consistency

functions.name: synchronize_groupkeyset_list

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node whose keysets are being synchronized

functions.description: Synchronizes datastore's keyset list with actual node's installed group keys.
functions.algorithm_detail: Read group keys from node using read_keysets_from_node(). For each keyset in datastore NodeKeySetList with Status=Pending: push to node using push_keyset_to_node(), mark Committed if success. For remaining keysets: replace datastore entries with node's actual keysets to ensure consistency.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T007, T015 during RefreshNode for cryptographic key synchronization

functions.name: synchronize_acl_list

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node whose ACLs are being synchronized

functions.description: Synchronizes datastore's ACL list with actual node's ACL attribute.
functions.algorithm_detail: Read ACL attribute from node using read_acls_from_node(). For each ACL in datastore ACLList with Status=Pending: push to node using push_acl_to_node(), mark Committed if success. For remaining ACLs: replace datastore entries with node's actual ACLs to ensure access control consistency.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T007, T015 during RefreshNode to synchronize access control policies

functions.name: remove_node_from_list

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node to be removed from datastore

functions.description: Removes Node Information Entry from NodeList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate entry in NodeList with matching NodeID. Remove entry from list, freeing associated memory. Update datastore indices if necessary.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T008, T017 when node removed after commissioning failure or deliberate removal

functions.name: ensure_node_has_keyset_for_group
functions.description: Ensures node has cryptographic keyset required for group membership.
functions.algorithm_detail: Look up GroupKeySetID for given GroupID in GroupList. Check if NodeKeySetList for NodeID contains entry for this GroupKeySetID. If not present: create new entry with Status=Pending, call push_keyset_to_node() to distribute key, mark Committed if successful.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T009 when adding node to group to ensure cryptographic prerequisites

functions.name: add_groupid_to_endpoint_groupidlist
functions.description: Adds GroupID entry to endpoint's GroupIDList in datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate endpoint entry in NodeList[NodeID].EndpointList matching EndpointID. Create new DatastoreGroupIDEntry with GroupID and StatusEntry.State=Status. Insert into endpoint's GroupIDList. Set UpdateTimestamp to current time.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T009 when adding endpoint to group membership

functions.name: push_groupid_to_endpoint
functions.description: Pushes group membership update to actual node's Groups cluster on specified endpoint.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke AddGroup command on node's Groups cluster for specified endpoint with GroupID and associated GroupKeySet. Uses Matter Interaction Model to send command. Returns true if command succeeds (SUCCESS status), false if command fails (BUSY, FAILURE, timeout, etc.).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T009 to propagate group membership to physical node

functions.name: if_result_success_set_status_committed

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node whose entry status may change

parameters.name: EndpointID
parameters.description: Endpoint whose entry status may change

parameters.name: GroupID
parameters.description: Group entry to potentially mark committed

functions.description: Conditional action: if previous push operation succeeded, marks entry as Committed.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check result of most recent push_groupid_to_endpoint operation. If result was success (true), locate corresponding GroupIDEntry in datastore and update StatusEntry.State to Committed. If result was failure (false), leave status as Pending for future retry.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T009 to update datastore state based on node operation outcome

functions.name: add_acl_to_acllist
functions.description: Adds ACL entry to node's ACLList in datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate node entry in NodeList. Generate unique ListID for this ACL entry. Create DatastoreACLEntry with ListID, ACLEntry, and StatusEntry.State=Status. Insert into node's ACLList. Set UpdateTimestamp.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T010 when adding access control entry to node

functions.name: push_acl_to_node
functions.description: Pushes ACL entry to actual node's Access Control cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke write operation on node's Access Control cluster ACL attribute. Append ACLEntry to existing ACL list. Uses Matter Interaction Model with appropriate access control checks. Returns true if write succeeds, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T010 to propagate access control policy to physical node

functions.name: add_binding_to_bindinglist
functions.description: Adds binding entry to endpoint's BindingList in datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate endpoint in NodeList[NodeID].EndpointList. Generate unique ListID. Create DatastoreBindingEntry with ListID, Binding, StatusEntry.State=Status. Insert into endpoint's BindingList.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T011 when adding binding to endpoint

functions.name: push_binding_to_endpoint
functions.description: Pushes binding to actual node's Binding cluster on specified endpoint.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke write operation on node's Binding cluster Binding attribute for specified endpoint. Add TargetStruct to binding list. Returns true if successful, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T011 to configure control relationships on physical node

functions.name: set_groupid_status_delete_pending
functions.description: Marks GroupID entry as DeletePending in datastore, preparing for removal.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate GroupIDEntry in NodeList[NodeID].EndpointList[EndpointID].GroupIDList matching GroupID. Update StatusEntry.State to DeletePending. Set UpdateTimestamp.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T012 when initiating group membership removal

functions.name: remove_groupid_from_endpoint
functions.description: Removes group membership from actual node's Groups cluster on specified endpoint.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke RemoveGroup command on node's Groups cluster for specified endpoint with GroupID. Uses Matter Interaction Model. Returns true if removal succeeds, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T012 to remove group membership from physical node

functions.name: if_result_success_delete_groupid_entry

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node whose entry may be deleted

parameters.name: EndpointID
parameters.description: Endpoint whose entry may be deleted

functions.description: Conditional action: if previous removal succeeded, deletes entry from datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check result of most recent remove_groupid_from_endpoint operation. If successful, locate and remove GroupIDEntry from endpoint's GroupIDList in datastore. If failed, leave entry with DeletePending status for retry.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T012 to clean up datastore after successful node update

functions.name: check_and_remove_unused_keyset

parameters.name: GroupID
parameters.description: Group whose keyset may be unused

functions.description: Checks if keyset for removed group is still used by other endpoints on node, removes if unused.
functions.algorithm_detail: Look up GroupKeySetID for GroupID. Iterate through all endpoints on NodeID checking if any GroupIDList references a group using same GroupKeySetID. If no other endpoint uses this keyset: mark NodeKeySetList entry as DeletePending, call remove_keyset_from_node(), delete entry if successful.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T012 to clean up unused cryptographic material after group removal

functions.name: set_acl_status_delete_pending
functions.description: Marks ACL entry as DeletePending in datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate ACL entry in NodeList[NodeID].ACLList with matching ListID. Update StatusEntry.State to DeletePending.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T013 when initiating ACL removal

functions.name: remove_acl_from_node
functions.description: Removes ACL entry from actual node's Access Control cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke write operation on node's Access Control cluster to remove ACL entry with matching ListID. Returns true if removal succeeds, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T013 to revoke access control from physical node

functions.name: if_result_success_delete_acl_entry

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node whose ACL may be deleted

functions.description: Conditional action: if ACL removal succeeded, deletes entry from datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check result of most recent remove_acl_from_node operation. If successful, remove ACL entry from NodeList[NodeID].ACLList. If failed, leave as DeletePending.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T013 to update datastore after access control revocation

functions.name: set_binding_status_delete_pending
functions.description: Marks binding entry as DeletePending in datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate binding in NodeList[NodeID].EndpointList[EndpointID].BindingList with matching ListID. Update StatusEntry.State to DeletePending.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T014 when initiating binding removal

functions.name: remove_binding_from_endpoint
functions.description: Removes binding from actual node's Binding cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke write operation on node's Binding cluster to remove binding entry with matching ListID. Returns true if successful, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T014 to remove control relationship from physical node

functions.name: if_result_success_delete_binding_entry

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: Node whose binding may be deleted

parameters.name: EndpointID
parameters.description: Endpoint whose binding may be deleted

functions.description: Conditional action: if binding removal succeeded, deletes entry from datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check result of most recent remove_binding_from_endpoint. If successful, remove binding entry from endpoint's BindingList in datastore. If failed, leave as DeletePending.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T014 to clean up datastore after binding removal

functions.name: log_synchronization_failure
functions.description: Records synchronization failure for diagnostic and retry purposes.
functions.algorithm_detail: Log error indicating node unreachable or update failed. Record timestamp, failure reason (timeout, BUSY, network error), retry count. Used for diagnostics and determining retry backoff strategy.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T016 when RefreshNode cannot reach node

functions.name: schedule_periodic_refresh
functions.description: Schedules future retry of RefreshNode operation for unreachable node.
functions.algorithm_detail: Add NodeID to pending retry queue. Set next retry time based on implementation-defined backoff strategy. Datastore will periodically process retry queue and attempt RefreshNode on queued nodes.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T016, T041, T043 to implement eventual consistency through retries

functions.name: create_group_entry

parameters.name: GroupCAT
parameters.description: CAT tag for group access control

functions.description: Creates new Group Information Entry in datastore.
functions.algorithm_detail: Allocate DatastoreGroupInformationEntry structure. Populate all fields with provided values. Insert into GroupList.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T021 when AddGroup command creates new group

functions.name: add_group_to_grouplist
functions.description: Inserts group entry into GroupList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Append GroupInformationEntry to GroupList attribute. Update datastore indices.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T021 during group creation

functions.name: update_group_fields

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Fields to update (FriendlyName, GroupCAT, GroupCATVersion, GroupPermission)

functions.description: Updates specified fields in existing group entry.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate group in GroupList with matching GroupID. For each non-null field parameter, update corresponding field in GroupInformationEntry. Null parameters are ignored (not updated).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T023, T025 when UpdateGroup modifies group properties

functions.name: propagate_group_update_to_members
functions.description: Distributes group property updates to all member nodes.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through NodeList finding all nodes with GroupID in any endpoint's GroupIDList. For FriendlyName changes: update group name on each member's Groups cluster. For CAT/Permission changes: update ACL entries referencing this group. Mark entries as Pending, attempt push to nodes, mark Committed if successful.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T023 to ensure all members see consistent group properties

functions.name: for_each_member_node_add_new_keyset_pending
functions.description: Adds new keyset with Pending status to all nodes that are members of specified group.
functions.algorithm_detail: Identify all nodes with GroupID in any GroupIDList. For each such node: add entry to NodeKeySetList with new_GroupKeySetID and Status=Pending. Attempt push_keyset_to_node(), mark Committed if successful. This implements first phase of group key rotation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T024 during group keyset rotation

functions.name: for_each_member_node_set_old_keyset_delete_pending
functions.description: Marks old keyset as DeletePending on all member nodes, preparing for removal.
functions.algorithm_detail: Identify all nodes that are members of GroupID. For each node: locate NodeKeySetList entry with old_GroupKeySetID, mark Status=DeletePending. Attempt remove_keyset_from_node(), delete entry if successful. This implements second phase of group key rotation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T024 to remove old keys after distributing new keys

functions.name: for_each_member_node_update_acls_pending
functions.description: Updates ACL entries referencing group on all member nodes, marking as Pending.
functions.algorithm_detail: Identify all nodes with ACL entries referencing GroupID (via CAT or GroupID field). For each such ACL: update with new GroupCAT, GroupCATVersion, or GroupPermission values. Mark Status=Pending. Attempt push_acl_to_node(), mark Committed if successful. This ensures access control changes propagate to members.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T025 when group's CAT properties change, implementing revocation mechanism

functions.name: set_groupid_status_delete_pending_on_nodes
functions.description: Marks all occurrences of GroupID across all nodes as DeletePending.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through all nodes in NodeList. For each endpoint's GroupIDList: if contains GroupID, mark StatusEntry.State=DeletePending. This prepares for group-wide membership removal.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T026 when group removal process begins

functions.name: remove_group_from_grouplist
functions.description: Deletes group entry from GroupList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate and remove GroupInformationEntry with matching GroupID from GroupList. Free associated memory.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T029 when group fully removed and no longer referenced

functions.name: add_keyset_to_keysetlist
functions.description: Adds new keyset to GroupKeySetList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Insert GroupKeySetStruct into GroupKeySetList attribute. GroupKeySet contains GroupKeySetID, SecurityPolicy, EpochKeys with timestamps.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T032 when AddKeySet command adds new cryptographic keyset

functions.name: update_keyset_in_keysetlist
functions.description: Updates existing keyset in GroupKeySetList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate keyset with matching GroupKeySetID in GroupKeySetList. Replace with new GroupKeySet fields (EpochKeys, SecurityPolicy, timestamps).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T035 when UpdateKeySet modifies keyset

functions.name: for_each_node_using_keyset_set_pending
functions.description: Marks keyset as Pending on all nodes that have it in NodeKeySetList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through NodeList. For each node with NodeKeySetList entry matching GroupKeySetID: update StatusEntry.State=Pending. This prepares for key update propagation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T035 to mark key distribution as pending update

functions.name: for_each_node_using_keyset_push_update
functions.description: Pushes updated keyset to all nodes that use it.
functions.algorithm_detail: For each node with NodeKeySetList entry for GroupKeySetID: call push_keyset_to_node() with updated keyset. If successful, mark Status=Committed. If failed, leave as Pending for retry.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T035 to distribute key updates across fabric

functions.name: remove_keyset_from_keysetlist
functions.description: Removes keyset from GroupKeySetList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate and remove GroupKeySetStruct with matching GroupKeySetID from GroupKeySetList. Free associated memory.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T036 when keyset no longer needed

functions.name: push_keyset_to_node
functions.description: Distributes cryptographic keyset to node's Group Key Management cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke KeySetWrite command on node's Group Key Management cluster with GroupKeySet. This installs epoch keys on node for deriving operational group keys. Returns true if successful, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during keyset distribution and updates to provision cryptographic material

functions.name: remove_keyset_from_node
functions.description: Removes keyset from node's Group Key Management cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke KeySetRemove command on node's Group Key Management cluster with GroupKeySetID. Returns true if removal successful, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when node no longer needs keyset after group removal

functions.name: read_partslist_from_node
functions.description: Reads list of endpoints from node's Descriptor cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke read operation on node's root endpoint (0) Descriptor cluster PartsList attribute. Returns list of endpoint IDs present on node. Used to discover actual endpoint topology.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in synchronize_endpoint_list() to determine actual endpoints

functions.name: read_keysets_from_node
functions.description: Reads installed keysets from node's Group Key Management cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Read GroupKeyMap attribute from node's Group Key Management cluster. Returns list of GroupKeySetStructs currently installed on node. Used to synchronize datastore with actual node state.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in synchronize_groupkeyset_list() during RefreshNode

functions.name: read_acls_from_node
functions.description: Reads ACL entries from node's Access Control cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Read ACL attribute from node's Access Control cluster. Returns list of AccessControlEntryStruct currently enforced on node. Used to synchronize access control policies.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in synchronize_acl_list() during RefreshNode

functions.name: read_grouplist_from_node
functions.description: Reads group memberships from endpoint's Groups cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Read GroupTable attribute from specified endpoint's Groups cluster. Returns list of GroupIDs this endpoint is a member of. Used to verify group membership synchronization.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during endpoint synchronization in RefreshNode

functions.name: read_bindinglist_from_node
functions.description: Reads bindings from endpoint's Binding cluster.
functions.algorithm_detail: Read Binding attribute from specified endpoint's Binding cluster. Returns list of TargetStruct representing control relationships. Used to verify binding synchronization.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during endpoint synchronization in RefreshNode

functions.name: generate_constraint_error
functions.description: Generates CONSTRAINT_ERROR response for constraint violations.
functions.algorithm_detail: Create error response with status code CONSTRAINT_ERROR. Include error reason in response. Used when operation would violate datastore integrity constraints (duplicate ID, resource in use, IPK removal).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in error transitions T006, T018, T022, T030, T033, T037, T038 for constraint violations

functions.name: generate_not_found_error
functions.description: Generates NOT_FOUND response when entity doesn't exist.
functions.algorithm_detail: Create error response with status code NOT_FOUND. Include entity type and ID in response. Used when operation references non-existent node, group, or keyset.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in error transitions T020, T031, T039 when entity lookup fails

functions.name: generate_invalid_constraint_error
functions.description: Generates INVALID_CONSTRAINT response for invalid operations.
functions.algorithm_detail: Create error response with status code INVALID_CONSTRAINT. Used when operation is logically invalid (e.g., adding already-existing node).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T006 when AddPendingNode called on existing node

functions.name: generate_resource_exhausted_error
functions.description: Generates RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED response when storage full.
functions.algorithm_detail: Create error response with status code RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. Indicates datastore storage capacity reached.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T004 when add operation fails due to storage limits

functions.name: notify_user_storage_exhausted
functions.description: Notifies user via proprietary means that storage capacity reached.
functions.algorithm_detail: Trigger notification mechanism (email, screen notification, etc.) to inform user that datastore storage limit reached. User must purge unused node information to continue operations. Notification mechanism is implementation-specific, outside specification scope.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T004 to alert user of critical storage condition

functions.name: current_time
functions.description: Returns current UTC timestamp.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query system clock and return current time in UTC format. Used to populate UpdateTimestamp fields in status entries.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called throughout FSM to timestamp state changes

functions.name: generate_event
functions.description: Generates event for logging, notification, or triggering dependent actions.
functions.algorithm_detail: Create event record with event_name and timestamp. Publish to event system for logging and notification. Events include: fabric_ready, node_pending_added, node_committed, node_removed, group_added, group_updated, keyset_added, etc.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called throughout FSM to signal state changes and important operations

functions.name: is_anchor_administrator
functions.description: Verifies caller is the anchor administrator with index 0.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if caller's NodeID matches AdminList[0].NodeID. Returns true if caller is anchor administrator, false otherwise. This is primary access control check for datastore operations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions throughout FSM to enforce anchor-only access

functions.name: has_administrator_CAT
functions.description: Verifies caller's NOC contains Administrator CAT tag.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse caller's Node Operational Certificate (NOC). Extract CAT (CASE Authenticated Tags) field. Check if contains Administrator CAT value. Returns true if Administrator CAT present, false otherwise. This enforces admin-level access control.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to verify caller has administrative privileges

functions.name: node_exists
functions.description: Checks if node entry exists in NodeList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search NodeList for entry with matching NodeID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to validate node references

functions.name: endpoint_exists
functions.description: Checks if endpoint exists in node's EndpointList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate node in NodeList. Search node's EndpointList for entry with matching EndpointID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions for endpoint-related operations

functions.name: group_exists
functions.description: Checks if group entry exists in GroupList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search GroupList for entry with matching GroupID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to validate group references

functions.name: keyset_exists
functions.description: Checks if keyset exists in GroupKeySetList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search GroupKeySetList for entry with matching GroupKeySetID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to validate keyset references

functions.name: keyset_in_use
functions.description: Checks if keyset is referenced by any groups or nodes (not DeletePending).
functions.algorithm_detail: Search GroupList for any group with matching GroupKeySetID. Search all nodes' NodeKeySetList for entries with matching GroupKeySetID that do not have Status=DeletePending. Returns true if any references found, false if keyset unused.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T036, T037 to prevent removing in-use keysets

functions.name: group_in_use
functions.description: Checks if group is referenced in any node's GroupIDList (not DeletePending).
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through all nodes' endpoints. For each endpoint's GroupIDList: check if contains entry with matching GroupID and Status != DeletePending. Returns true if any active references found, false if group unused.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T029, T030 to prevent removing in-use groups

functions.name: commissioning_completed_successfully
functions.description: Checks if node commissioning completed successfully.
functions.algorithm_detail: Verify node has completed CASE session establishment, obtained NOC, and is reachable. Returns true if commissioning succeeded, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T007 to transition from Pending to Committed

functions.name: commissioning_failed
functions.description: Checks if node commissioning failed.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if CASE session failed, NOC issuance failed, or node became unreachable during commissioning. Returns true if commissioning failed, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T008 to remove failed commissioning attempts

functions.name: all_acls_removed
functions.description: Checks if node has no ACL entries remaining.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if NodeList[NodeID].ACLList is empty. Returns true if no ACLs, false if any ACL entries exist.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions T017, T018 to enforce ACL-removal-before-node-removal requirement

functions.name: has_pending_or_delete_pending_entries
functions.description: Checks if node has any entries with Pending or DeletePending status.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through node's NodeKeySetList, ACLList, and all EndpointList entries (GroupIDList, BindingList). Return true if any entry has Status=Pending or Status=DeletePending, false if all Committed.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T015 to determine if RefreshNode needed

functions.name: node_unreachable
functions.description: Checks if node is currently unreachable on network.
functions.algorithm_detail: Attempt to establish CASE session with node. Returns true if connection times out, node offline, or network error. Returns false if node responds.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T016 to handle temporary network failures

functions.name: storage_capacity_reached
functions.description: Checks if datastore storage limit reached.
functions.algorithm_detail: Compare current datastore size (number of entries) against implementation-defined storage limit. Returns true if at or above limit, false if capacity available.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T004 to prevent operations when storage full

functions.name: storage_capacity_available
functions.description: Checks if storage capacity now available after purge.
functions.algorithm_detail: Compare current datastore size against storage limit. Returns true if below limit (space available), false if still at limit.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T045 to resume operations after storage freed

functions.name: groupid_exists_in_endpoint
functions.description: Checks if GroupID exists in endpoint's GroupIDList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate endpoint in node's EndpointList. Search GroupIDList for entry with matching GroupID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T012 to validate group removal operation

functions.name: acl_exists_in_node
functions.description: Checks if ACL entry with given ListID exists in node's ACLList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search node's ACLList for entry with matching ListID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T013 to validate ACL removal operation

functions.name: binding_exists_in_endpoint
functions.description: Checks if binding entry with given ListID exists in endpoint's BindingList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate endpoint in node's EndpointList. Search BindingList for entry with matching ListID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T014 to validate binding removal operation

functions.name: all_delete_pending_members_removed
functions.description: Checks if all nodes with DeletePending status for this group have completed removal.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search all nodes' GroupIDLists for entries with matching GroupID and Status=DeletePending. Returns true if none found, false if any remain.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions T027, T028 to determine when group removal process complete

functions.name: still_has_active_members
functions.description: Checks if group still has nodes with Committed or Pending membership.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search all nodes' GroupIDLists for entries with matching GroupID and Status=Committed or Status=Pending. Returns true if any found, false if none.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T027 to determine group's continued existence

functions.name: no_active_members
functions.description: Checks if group has zero members (all removed).
functions.algorithm_detail: Search all nodes' GroupIDLists for entries with matching GroupID (any status). Returns true if none found, false if any members exist.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition for T028 to identify empty groups

functions.name: log_update_failure
functions.description: Logs failure to update entry on node for diagnostics.
functions.algorithm_detail: Record entry identifier, node ID, failure reason, timestamp. Used for troubleshooting persistent synchronization failures.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T041 when node update fails

functions.name: schedule_retry_on_next_refresh
functions.description: Marks entry for retry during next RefreshNode operation.
functions.algorithm_detail: Entry remains in Pending or DeletePending state. When RefreshNode next executes for this node, entry will be retried. This implements eventual consistency.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T041, T043 to implement retry mechanism

functions.name: remove_entry_from_datastore
functions.description: Deletes entry from appropriate datastore list after successful node removal.
functions.algorithm_detail: Based on entry type (KeySet, ACL, GroupID, Binding), locate and remove from corresponding list in datastore. Free associated memory.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T042 when DeletePending entry successfully removed from node

functions.name: log_removal_failure
functions.description: Logs failure to remove entry from node for diagnostics.
functions.algorithm_detail: Record entry identifier, node ID, failure reason, timestamp. Used for troubleshooting persistent removal failures.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T043 when node removal operation fails

functions.name: for_each_pending_entry_attempt_node_update
functions.description: Periodic operation to retry all Pending entry updates across all nodes.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through all nodes in NodeList. For each node: collect all entries (KeySets, ACLs, GroupIDs, Bindings) with Status=Pending. Attempt to push each to node. Mark Committed if successful. This implements periodic retry for temporary failures.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T044 during periodic pending review

functions.name: for_each_delete_pending_entry_attempt_node_removal
functions.description: Periodic operation to retry all DeletePending entry removals across all nodes.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through all nodes in NodeList. For each node: collect all entries with Status=DeletePending. Attempt to remove each from node. Delete from datastore if successful. This implements periodic retry for removal operations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T044 during periodic pending review

functions.name: update_admin_fields
functions.description: Updates administrator information in AdminList. Null parameters are ignored.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate admin entry in AdminList with matching NodeID. For each non-null parameter: update corresponding field in AdministratorInformationEntry. If ICAC updated, may require updating AnchorRootCA if this is anchor administrator.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T046 when UpdateAdmin modifies administrator information

functions.name: remove_admin_from_list
functions.description: Removes administrator entry from AdminList. Cannot remove anchor administrator.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate and remove AdministratorInformationEntry with matching NodeID from AdminList. Update indices for remaining administrators. Free associated memory.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T048 when RemoveAdmin removes non-anchor administrator

functions.name: is_anchor_administrator_by_nodeid
functions.description: Checks if given NodeID belongs to anchor administrator (index 0).
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if AdminList[0].NodeID matches given NodeID. Returns true if anchor administrator, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard condition T049 to prevent anchor administrator removal

functions.name: admin_exists
functions.description: Checks if administrator entry exists in AdminList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search AdminList for entry with matching NodeID. Returns true if found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions T046, T047, T048, T050 to validate admin operations

functions.name: update_endpoint_friendlyname
functions.description: Updates FriendlyName field for endpoint in node's EndpointList.
functions.algorithm_detail: Locate endpoint entry in NodeList[NodeID].EndpointList matching EndpointID. Update FriendlyName field with new value.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T051 when UpdateEndpointForNode modifies endpoint name

definitions.definition: A Matter fabric shared across multiple ecosystems with multiple Joint Fabric Administrators. Enables inter-ecosystem device control and coordination.
definitions.security_relevance: Joint Fabric extends trust across vendor boundaries, requiring careful access control and revocation mechanisms to prevent cross-ecosystem privilege escalation.

definitions.definition: The Joint Fabric Administrator at index 0 in AdminList that serves as the Anchor Root CA and controls the root of trust for the fabric.
definitions.security_relevance: Single point of authority for fabric trust. Compromise of Anchor Administrator enables complete fabric takeover. Must be uniquely identified and protected.

definitions.definition: Identifier embedded in Node Operational Certificate used for group-based access control. Each group has unique CAT value and version number.
definitions.security_relevance: CAT provides scalable access control without per-node ACLs. Revocation achieved by incrementing CAT version, invalidating all previous NOCs with old version.

definitions.definition: Monotonically increasing version number associated with group's CAT. Incremented when member removed to invalidate their credentials.
definitions.security_relevance: Core revocation mechanism. Failure to increment enables removed members to retain access indefinitely. Must be strictly monotonic to prevent replay.

definitions.definition: Special keyset with ID 0 that must exist in all fabrics. Used to protect node identities in Matter communications.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical for privacy. Removal exposes all node identities to eavesdroppers. Must never be deleted. Compromise enables identity tracking across fabric.

definitions.definition: Structure containing epoch keys used to derive operational group keys for encrypted group communication.
definitions.security_relevance: Cryptographic material enabling group confidentiality and authentication. Must be distributed only to authorized members. Updates must be atomic across group.

definitions.definition: State indicating operation initiated in datastore but not yet confirmed on target node. Awaits synchronization.
definitions.security_relevance: Asymmetric state between datastore and node creates window for security vulnerabilities. Pending ACL additions may not yet protect, pending removals may still grant access.

definitions.definition: State indicating datastore and node are synchronized. Operation completed successfully.
definitions.security_relevance: Only Committed state guarantees security properties are enforced. Administrators must verify Committed status before trusting security configurations.

definitions.definition: State indicating removal initiated in datastore, awaiting confirmation from node.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical for revocation. Removed credentials/keys may still be valid on node until DeletePending resolves to deletion. Creates revocation lag window.

definitions.definition: Command that synchronizes all pending and delete-pending entries between datastore and node. Pushes pending additions, removes delete-pending entries, validates committed entries.
definitions.security_relevance: THE critical security synchronization point. All security updates (ACLs, keys, group memberships) reach nodes via RefreshNode. Failure blocks revocations and access grants.

definitions.definition: Long-term key material in GroupKeySet from which operational group keys are derived using KDF.
definitions.security_relevance: Root secret for group cryptography. Compromise enables decryption of all group communications. Must be rotated if leaked.

definitions.definition: Session keys derived from epoch keys using KDF with fabric-specific salts. Used for actual AES-CCM encryption/authentication of group messages.
definitions.security_relevance: Derived keys enable group communication. Derivation must be deterministic so all members compute same keys. KDF must be cryptographically secure.

definitions.definition: Node with administrative privileges over Joint Fabric, identified by Administrator CAT in NOC. Can access datastore to manage nodes and groups across multiple ecosystems.
definitions.security_relevance: Administrators have privileged access. Must be carefully authenticated. Multiple administrators can coexist but anchor administrator has unique authority.

definitions.definition: Special CAT value identifying nodes with Joint Fabric Administrator privileges.
definitions.security_relevance: Grants unrestricted access to datastore. Must be carefully controlled. Only trusted administrators should receive NOCs with Administrator CAT.

definitions.definition: Certificate used by administrators to sign Node Operational Certificates (NOCs). Itself signed by Anchor Root CA.
definitions.security_relevance: Establishes chain of trust from Anchor to NOCs. Compromise enables rogue NOC issuance. Must be properly validated and protected.

definitions.definition: X.509 certificate containing node's public key and CAT tags, signed by administrator's ICAC. Used for CASE authentication.
definitions.security_relevance: Node's identity and access control credentials. Embedded CATs determine group memberships and privileges. Theft enables impersonation.

definitions.definition: Property that datastore and nodes will eventually synchronize despite temporary failures, through periodic retry of pending operations.
definitions.security_relevance: Security updates may be delayed but will eventually propagate. Creates temporal windows where security policies not yet enforced. Critical that retries succeed eventually.

security_properties.description: Only the node designated as Anchor Administrator (index 0 in AdminList) can access and modify the datastore.
critical_transitions: All command transitions require is_anchor_administrator guard

security_properties.description: All admin-level operations on datastore require caller to present valid Administrator CAT in their NOC.
critical_transitions: All command transitions require has_administrator_CAT guard

security_properties.description: All node updates follow Pending → Committed workflow, ensuring atomicity between datastore and physical node state.
Identity Protection Key (GroupKeySetID 0) cannot be removed from GroupKeySetList, protecting fabric-wide identity privacy.
When node removed from group, GroupCATVersion increments, invalidating all NOCs with old CAT version.
critical_transitions: T025 (CAT version update), T026 (member removal with CAT increment)

security_properties.description: Entries marked DeletePending are removed from nodes during RefreshNode, ensuring revocations eventually complete.
KeySets in use by groups or nodes cannot be deleted, preserving cryptographic continuity.
critical_transitions: T037 (keyset removal blocked if in use)

security_properties.description: Groups with active members cannot be deleted, preventing orphaned group references.
critical_transitions: T030 (group removal blocked if in use)

security_properties.description: Nodes reach Committed state only via successful RefreshNode after commissioning completes.
When group properties updated, changes propagate to all member nodes (keys, ACLs, CAT versions).
All ACLs must be removed from node before node entry can be deleted from datastore, preventing stale ACL references.
Failed updates remain Pending and are retried periodically until success, guaranteeing eventual synchronization.
When storage exhausted, no new entries added and user notified, preventing uncontrolled resource exhaustion.

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: GroupKeySet distribution via Matter Group Key Management cluster
cryptographic_operations.output: IPK installed on node for identity protection
assumptions: KDF used for key derivation is secure (HKDF-SHA256)
IPK epoch keys have sufficient entropy
Secure channel (CASE) protects IPK during distribution

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: GroupKeySet distribution via KeySetWrite command to Group Key Management cluster
cryptographic_operations.output: Epoch keys installed on node for group operational key derivation
assumptions: Epoch keys generated with cryptographically secure random number generator
CASE session protects keys in transit

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: HKDF-SHA256 key derivation from epoch keys
cryptographic_operations.output: 128-bit operational group key for AES-CCM encryption
assumptions: All group members perform identical derivation

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: X.509 certificate generation with CAT in Subject field
cryptographic_operations.output: Node Operational Certificate (NOC) with embedded CAT
assumptions: Certificate validation performed on every CASE session

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: X.509 chain validation from NOC through ICAC to Anchor Root CA
assumptions: Certificates not revoked (via CAT version check)

cryptographic_operations.output: Secure session keys for encrypted communication

security_assumptions.assumption: Anchor Administrator node is not compromised and protects its private keys securely
security_assumptions.if_violated: Complete fabric takeover. Attacker can sign arbitrary NOCs, create rogue administrators, modify datastore arbitrarily, and distribute malicious keys.

security_assumptions.assumption: Administrator CAT credentials cannot be forged or stolen without detection
security_assumptions.if_violated: Non-administrator nodes gain datastore access, enabling unauthorized topology manipulation, ACL modification, and key distribution.

security_assumptions.assumption: Network communication between Datastore and nodes is eventually reliable (retries succeed)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Permanent inconsistency between datastore and nodes. Security updates never reach nodes, revocations incomplete, key rotations fail.

security_assumptions.assumption: Nodes correctly report their configuration via Matter clusters (Descriptor, Groups, ACL, Binding)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Datastore synchronization produces incorrect state. Malicious nodes can hide unauthorized configurations or appear compliant while violating policies.

security_assumptions.assumption: CAT versions are monotonically increasing and never reused within group lifetime
security_assumptions.if_violated: CAT version rollover allows revoked NOCs to become valid again. Removed group members regain access after version wraps around.

security_assumptions.assumption: NodeIDs not reused within timeframe where stale references could exist
security_assumptions.if_violated: Newly commissioned node with recycled NodeID inherits stale ACL grants intended for previous node, gaining unauthorized access.

security_assumptions.assumption: HKDF-SHA256 is cryptographically secure for operational key derivation
security_assumptions.if_violated: Operational group keys predictable or derivable without epoch key. Attackers decrypt group communications or forge authenticated messages.

security_assumptions.assumption: Epoch keys generated with sufficient entropy from secure random source
security_assumptions.if_violated: Weak epoch keys enable brute-force attacks. Predictable keys allow non-members to derive operational group keys and access group communications.

security_assumptions.assumption: CASE protocol secures key distribution channel against eavesdropping and tampering
security_assumptions.if_violated: Epoch keys and IPK leaked during distribution. Attacker intercepts keys, enabling passive decryption of all group traffic.

security_assumptions.assumption: Administrators coordinate to avoid simultaneous conflicting operations on same resources
security_assumptions.if_violated: Race conditions produce inconsistent state. Last-write-wins causes security updates to be lost. ACL revocations overwritten, keys incorrectly distributed.

security_assumptions.assumption: Datastore storage capacity sufficient for expected number of nodes and groups
security_assumptions.if_violated: RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED errors block critical security operations. New nodes cannot be added, key rotations fail, revocations incomplete.

security_assumptions.assumption: X.509 certificate validation correctly enforces signature verification and chain of trust
security_assumptions.if_violated: Rogue NOCs accepted. Attackers with invalid certificates gain fabric access. Certificate forgery enables complete authentication bypass.

validation_checklist.note: No explicit timers in specification - periodic operations are implementation-defined. Retry mechanisms exist but no hard timing constraints specified.
